_meta.notes: Per-sport facts generated by scripts/generate-sport-facts.ts using Perplexity sonar-pro grounded on Paris 2024 medal results.

Ceremony.gamesContext: Ceremonies return to Los Angeles with an innovative dual-venue format for the Opening Ceremony, shared between the 2028 Stadium and LA Memorial Coliseum on July 14, 2028, marking the Coliseum's third Games appearance and the 2028 Stadium's debut. The Paralympic Opening Ceremony occurs solely at the 2028 Stadium on August 15, 2028, while the Paralympic Closing Ceremony takes place at the LA Memorial Coliseum on August 27, 2028. This setup blends historic legacy with modern spectacle, differing from single-venue traditions in prior Games.
Ceremony.parisRecap: This sport was not contested at Paris 2024, or medal data is unavailable.

Athletics (Track & Field).gamesContext: Athletics returns to the 2028 Games as a core sport with 48 medal events across track, field, relays, race walks, and multi-events for men, women, and mixed categories. The format remains a multi-day tournament of qualifying rounds, heats, semifinals, and finals, with track and field sessions concentrated in the first week from July 15-24. A new high-speed 4x100m mixed relay joins the program alongside the existing 4x400m mixed relay. No major changes from Paris 2024 beyond the addition of this sprint relay.

Athletics (Marathon).gamesContext: Marathon events return to the 2028 Games as established disciplines with no changes from Paris 2024, featuring one men's marathon and one women's marathon for a total of two medal events. Both races follow the standard 42.195 km distance on road courses. Notable storylines include East African dominance and potential repeat bids by Paris medalists.

Athletics (Race Walk).gamesContext: Race walk returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the mixed marathon race walk relay and new half-marathon race walk events for men and women, replacing the 20km individual events from Paris 2024. The program features two medal events in this discipline. This shift emphasizes longer distances and mixed team formats, continuing the evolution seen in recent Games.
Athletics (Race Walk).parisRecap: In Paris 2024, race walk events delivered standout performances including Brian Pintado of Ecuador winning the men's 20km in 1:18:55 ahead of Caio Bonfim of Brazil and Álvaro Martín of Spain, while Yang Jiayu of China took the women's 20km gold with María Pérez of Spain and Jemima Montag of Australia completing the podiums. The mixed marathon race walk relay saw Spain claim gold, followed by Ecuador and Australia. These results highlighted emerging talents from South America and Asia in a discipline marked by tight competition and national records.

Swimming.gamesContext: Swimming returns to the 2028 Summer Games with an expanded program introducing men's and women's 50m events in backstroke, breaststroke, and butterfly, alongside the traditional distances and relays. The competition spans nine days from July 22 to 30, featuring heats over the first eight days and a single finals session on the last day. It includes 37 medal events, with notable storylines around the new sprint additions and the final champions crowned just before the Closing Ceremony.

Artistic Gymnastics.gamesContext: Artistic Gymnastics returns to the 2028 Games as a core sport with the same 18 medal events as Paris 2024: men's and women's team all-around, individual all-around, and six apparatus finals each. Competition spans July 15-20 and July 22-25, featuring multi-day qualifications subdivided by gender and session, team finals, all-around finals, and apparatus finals. The mixed team final appears with format and duration pending FIG approval, potentially introducing a new element. No major format changes from Paris 2024 are noted.

Basketball.gamesContext: Basketball returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with separate men's and women's tournaments, each featuring a group phase followed by quarterfinals, semifinals, bronze medal game, and gold medal game for a total of six medal events. The format remains unchanged from Paris 2024, maintaining 5x5 full-court play. Notable storylines include the United States' bid to extend its dominance and potential challenges from European and Australian powerhouses.

Football (Soccer).gamesContext: Football (soccer) returns to the 2028 Games with both men's and women's tournaments featuring 16 teams each, maintaining the traditional format of group stage, knockout rounds, and medal matches. The tournament spans seven venues across the United States—six for preliminary and knockout matches and the Rose Bowl for final-stage matches—reflecting a distributed model prioritizing sustainability and regional access rather than centralized construction. The men's gold medal match is scheduled for July 28, 2028, and the women's gold medal match for July 29, 2028, at the Rose Bowl Stadium.
Football (Soccer).parisRecap: At Paris 2024, Spain claimed the men's gold medal, with France taking silver and Morocco bronze. The United States won the women's gold medal, defeating Brazil (silver), while Germany secured the bronze medal. These results set the stage for competitive rematches and emerging rivalries heading into the 2028 tournament.

Tennis.gamesContext: Tennis returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with the standard five events: men's and women's singles, men's and women's doubles, and mixed doubles. The tournament format progresses from round-robin or early rounds through quarterfinals, semifinals, and bronze/gold medal matches, awarding 5 gold medals total. No major format changes from Paris 2024; all events remain on outdoor hard courts.

Diving.gamesContext: Diving returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a longstanding sport with 16 medal events across men's and women's 3m springboard, 10m platform, and synchronized variants, following the same format as Paris 2024. All individual events feature preliminary, semifinal, and final rounds, while synchronized events culminate in a single final. This marks the first time in modern Games history that diving is separated from swimming, hosted at a dedicated aquatics center.

Volleyball.gamesContext: Volleyball returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with unchanged 12-team tournaments for both men and women, featuring 4 medal events per gender across preliminary rounds, quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches. The format remains consistent with Paris 2024, with preliminary rounds from July 15-23, quarterfinals on July 24-25, and knockout stages concluding by July 30. Men's competition starts first on July 15, followed by women on July 16.

Beach Volleyball.gamesContext: Beach volleyball returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a staple event with the same format as Paris 2024, featuring men's and women's tournaments each with 24 teams competing through preliminaries, round of 16, quarterfinals, semifinals, and bronze/gold medal matches for a total of 2 medal events per gender. Competition spans from July 15 to 29 at Alamitos Beach Stadium, with daily preliminary sessions and medal matches concluding the schedule. No changes to event structure or number of medals from Paris 2024.

Baseball.gamesContext: Baseball returns to the 2028 Summer Games after a 12-year absence, with the sport last featured at Tokyo 2020. The 2028 competition will feature a single men's tournament format with six medal events (though the primary medal is awarded to the gold medal winner, with silver and bronze also contested). The tournament structure includes opening round, quarterfinal, semifinal, and medal game rounds, continuing the traditional knockout-style format that has defined the sport at the Games.
Baseball.parisRecap: Baseball was not contested at the 2024 Paris Games. The sport's most recent appearance was at Tokyo 2020, where Japan won gold with a 2–0 victory over the United States, while the Dominican Republic secured bronze. Prior to that, the sport had been a regular fixture at the Summer Games, with Cuba historically dominating the medal count with three gold medals across the sport's Olympic history.

Softball.gamesContext: Softball returns to the 2028 Summer Games after its most recent appearance at Tokyo 2020, having debuted at Atlanta 1996. The tournament features women's teams competing in a preliminary round followed by bronze and gold medal games. Six medal events are awarded across the softball competition.
Softball.parisRecap: Softball was not contested at Paris 2024.

Flag Football.gamesContext: Flag Football makes its debut at the 2028 Summer Games as a new addition to the program. It features separate 6-team tournaments for men and women, each culminating in preliminaries, semifinals, bronze medal games, and gold medal games for a total of four medal events. The non-contact format emphasizes fast, explosive play on a 70-yard field with 20-minute halves.
Flag Football.parisRecap: Flag Football was not contested at Paris 2024.

Cricket.gamesContext: Cricket returns to the 2028 Summer Games for the first time since 1900, featuring separate men's and women's tournaments in T20 format with six teams each. Each competition follows a group stage with first and second rounds, culminating in bronze and gold medal matches. A total of four medal events will be contested, with all matches hosted as double-headers on most days starting July 12, medal matches on July 20 and 29.
Cricket.parisRecap: Cricket was not contested at Paris 2024.

Lacrosse.gamesContext: Lacrosse returns to the 2028 Summer Games for the first time since 1908 in a new fast-paced Lacrosse Sixes format with six players per team, smaller fields, four 8-minute quarters, and a 30-second shot clock. The tournament includes men's and women's qualifying rounds leading to bronze and gold medal games, for a total of four medal events. This adaptation emphasizes speed, high-scoring action, and global accessibility compared to traditional 10-a-side field lacrosse.
Lacrosse.parisRecap: Lacrosse was not contested at Paris 2024.

Squash.gamesContext: Squash makes its debut at the 2028 Summer Games as a medal sport, featuring both men's and women's competitions with equal representation. The format progresses from preliminary rounds through Round of 16, Quarterfinals, Semifinals, and medal matches, with 2 medal events (men's and women's singles). This inaugural inclusion marks a significant milestone for the sport's international recognition at the Games level.
Squash.parisRecap: Squash was not contested at Paris 2024 and therefore has no medal history from that Games. The 2028 Summer Games will mark squash's first appearance as a medal sport at the Games.

3x3 Basketball.gamesContext: 3x3 Basketball returns to the 2028 Summer Games after debuting in Tokyo 2020, with expanded men's and women's tournaments from 8 to 12 teams each, including play-in games for seeding. The format features play-ins, pool rounds, quarterfinals, semifinals, and bronze/gold medal games for both genders, spanning July 16-22. Two medal events per gender will be contested, totaling four medal sets.

Boxing - Finals.gamesContext: Boxing returns to the 2028 Games with full gender parity for the first time, featuring seven men's and seven women's weight classes for a total of 14 medal events. The format follows the standard tournament structure with preliminary bouts leading to semifinals and finals. This marks a continuation from Paris 2024 with no changes in the number of weight classes or overall structure.

Boxing - Prelims.gamesContext: Boxing returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with 13 men's and 6 women's weight classes contested in single-elimination tournament format from round of 32 through semifinals, awarding 19 medal events total. Preliminary stages including R32, R16, quarterfinals, and some semifinals occur early in the schedule. This maintains the structure from Paris 2024 with no changes to events or format announced.

Surfing.gamesContext: Surfing returns to the 2028 Summer Games after debuting in Tokyo 2020 and continuing in Paris 2024, featuring 2 medal events in Men's Shortboard and Women's Shortboard. The format follows a standard progression of rounds from Round 1 through quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches for both men and women. No changes from Paris 2024 are noted, maintaining the shortboard discipline.

Skateboarding.gamesContext: Skateboarding returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a mainstay following its debut in Tokyo 2020, featuring four medal events across men's and women's street and park disciplines with the same format as Paris 2024: preliminary rounds leading to best-trick finals in street and three-run finals in park. The street events include preliminary sessions to qualify top competitors for finals, maintaining the high-stakes, single-elimination style. Youth dominance persists as a key storyline, with teenagers claiming most Paris 2024 medals.

Skateboarding (Street).gamesContext: Skateboarding returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a mainstay since its debut in Tokyo 2020, featuring both Street and Park formats with four medal events each for men and women. The Street format emphasizes tricks on urban obstacles leading to qualifying rounds and finals. No changes to event structure from Paris 2024. The sport highlights its Southern California roots with events in the San Fernando Valley for the first time.

Skateboarding (Park).gamesContext: Skateboarding Park returns to the 2028 Summer Games as an established event following its debut in Tokyo 2020. The format features preliminary rounds advancing top skaters to finals for men and women, with two medal events total. No changes from Paris 2024 format are noted.
Skateboarding (Park).parisRecap: In Men's Park at Paris 2024, Keegan Palmer of Australia defended his title with a winning score of 93.11 ahead of Tom Schaar of USA in silver and Augusto Akio of Brazil in bronze, in a final marked by surprises where few completed initial runs. Women's Park saw Arisa Trew of Australia take gold, Kokona Hiraki of Japan silver, and Sky Brown of Great Britain bronze, continuing the trend of teenage dominance in skateboarding with medalists averaging young ages.

Climbing.gamesContext: Climbing returns to the 2028 Summer Games with a brand-new format separating the three disciplines—Boulder, Lead, and Speed—into standalone medal events for men and women, awarding six medals total, up from four combined events at Paris 2024. Competition spans six days from July 24 to 29, matching Paris duration but with an expanded athlete quota of 76 climbers. This evolution triples the medal count from the sport's debut at Tokyo 2020 and highlights its growing Olympic prominence.

Golf.gamesContext: Golf returns to the 2028 Summer Games after debuting in 2016, featuring men's and women's individual stroke play over four rounds each, plus a new mixed team event with a preliminary four-ball round and final foursomes. This introduces the first team competition since 1904, expanding from the two individual events in Paris 2024. Three medal events are contested: one each for men, women, and mixed team.

Rugby Sevens.gamesContext: Rugby Sevens returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core event since its debut in 2016, featuring separate men's and women's tournaments each with 12 teams in a pool play format leading to quarterfinals, semifinals, bronze, and gold medal matches for 6 total medal events. The women's tournament runs first from July 12-15, followed by the men's from July 16-18, with no format changes from Paris 2024. Notable storylines include growing popularity in North America as a launchpad for the sport's expansion.

Badminton.gamesContext: Badminton returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the same five medal events as Paris 2024: men's singles, men's doubles, women's singles, women's doubles, and mixed doubles. The format features group play stages followed by single-elimination knockout rounds including round of 16, quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches for each category. No changes to events or format are announced from Paris 2024, maintaining 5 medal events total.
Badminton.parisRecap: Viktor Axelsen of Denmark won men's singles gold over Kunlavut Vitidsarn of Thailand, with Lee Zii Jia of Malaysia taking bronze in a competitive field. Chinese Taipei's Lee Yang and Wang Chi-lin claimed men's doubles gold, defending their Tokyo title against China's Liang Weikeng and Wang Chang, while Malaysia's Aaron Chia and Soh Wooi Yik earned bronze. An Se-Yeong of South Korea dominated women's singles for gold ahead of China's He Bingjiao, with Indonesia's Gregoria Mariska Tunjung securing bronze; China swept women's doubles gold and silver with Chen Qingchen/Jia Yifan over Liu Shengshu/Tan Ning, Japan taking bronze, and China's Zheng Siwei/Huang Yaqiong won mixed doubles gold over South Korea's pair, with Japan earning bronze.

Judo.gamesContext: Judo returns to the 2028 Summer Games with 14 individual weight classes for men and women plus the mixed team event introduced at Tokyo 2020. Competition follows single-elimination format with repechage rounds leading to semifinals, medal contests, and golden score overtime if needed. The mixed team event features men and women competing together in best-of-seven bouts to promote gender equality and team strategy.

Fencing.gamesContext: Fencing returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the standard 12 medal events across men's and women's individual and team competitions in épée, foil, and sabre. The format follows the traditional direct-elimination structure with pool rounds leading to tables of 64, semifinals, and medal bouts for individuals, and team events featuring pools, table of 8, and bronze/gold finals. No changes from Paris 2024 are noted in event lineup or structure.

Taekwondo.gamesContext: Taekwondo returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the standard format of eight weight classes per gender, featuring single-elimination tournaments that include round of 32, round of 16, quarterfinals, semifinals, repechages for bronze medal contention, and gold medal contests. The program maintains 16 medal events total, unchanged from Paris 2024. Sessions span multiple days with progressive knockout stages building to medal finals.

Wrestling.gamesContext: Wrestling returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with 18 medal events across men's and women's freestyle and men's Greco-Roman categories, maintaining the format established since Tokyo 2020 with six weight classes per discipline. Sessions feature preliminary rounds like round of 16, quarterfinals, semifinals, and repechages leading to dedicated medal matches for each weight class. No major format changes from Paris 2024.

Handball.gamesContext: Handball returns to the 2028 Summer Games with separate men's and women's tournaments featuring preliminary rounds of multiple group matches, quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches for two medal events per gender. The format remains consistent with Paris 2024, including six preliminary games per day alternating between men and women until quarterfinals. Competition spans July 12-28 at Long Beach Arena, one of the longest schedules among sports.

Hockey.gamesContext: Field hockey returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core team sport with separate men's and women's tournaments, each featuring 12 teams in a pool stage followed by play-ins, quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches for a total of six medal events. The format remains consistent with Paris 2024, including two play-in matches per gender to determine the final quarterfinal spots. Notable storylines include the Netherlands' bid for a second straight double gold after dominating Paris, alongside rising challengers like India and Germany in the men's draw.
Hockey.parisRecap: In Paris 2024, the Netherlands claimed gold in both men's and women's field hockey, defeating Germany in the men's final and the People's Republic of China in the women's. India secured men's bronze with a dramatic penalty shootout victory over Spain, while Argentina took women's bronze against Belgium. The Dutch double triumph highlighted their ongoing dominance, with no major records broken but intense rivalries evident in close semifinal and bronze matches.

Water Polo.gamesContext: Water polo returns to the 2028 Games with historic gender parity: both men's and women's competitions feature 12-team tournaments, marking the first time the sport has achieved equal representation at this level. The tournament format progresses through preliminary rounds, quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches for both genders. Four medal events are contested—men's and women's gold, silver, and bronze matches. Serbia enters as defending men's champions from Paris 2024, while Spain holds the women's title.
Water Polo.parisRecap: Serbia captured the men's gold medal at Paris 2024, defeating Croatia in the final, with the United States securing bronze. Spain won the women's gold medal, edging Australia for first place, while the Netherlands claimed the bronze medal. These results set the stage for competitive rematches and medal pursuits at the 2028 Games.

Table Tennis.gamesContext: Table tennis returns to the 2028 Summer Games with singles, doubles, and mixed team events spanning 15 days from July 15 to July 29. The program features 12 medal events across men's singles, women's singles, men's doubles, women's doubles, mixed doubles, and mixed team competitions. This maintains the format from Paris 2024 with no announced changes to event structure.

Weightlifting.gamesContext: Weightlifting returns to the 2028 Games as a core sport with 10 medal events across men's and women's weight classes, featuring snatch and clean & jerk lifts in group finals format. The program maintains the Paris 2024 structure of 10 total weight categories, reduced from prior Games, with sessions structured as medal finals. Notable storylines include defending champions like Lasha Talakhadze and the emergence of young talents such as Rizki Juniansyah.

Archery.gamesContext: Archery returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the debut of the compound mixed team event alongside traditional recurve events in individual, team, and mixed team formats. The program features 12 medal events, including five recurve and one new compound mixed team. This marks the first inclusion of compound archery at the Games, expanding from the four recurve medal events in Paris 2024.
Archery.parisRecap: Republic of Korea dominated Paris 2024, securing gold in all five events: Men's Individual by Kim Woo-Jin over USA's Brady Ellison, Women's Individual by Kim Woo-Jin over Nam Su-Hyeon, Men's Team over France, Women's Team over China, and Mixed Team over Germany. France earned silver in Men's Team and bronze in Women's Individual via Lisa Barbelin, while the USA took bronze in Mixed Team. The events unfolded with qualification rounds followed by elimination brackets culminating in medal matches at Esplanade des Invalides.

Canoe Slalom.gamesContext: Canoe Slalom returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the same format as Paris 2024, featuring men's and women's kayak singles, canoe singles, and kayak cross events. The program includes six medal events across these disciplines, with kayak cross continuing as a full-contact head-to-head format introduced in Paris. No changes to the event structure are planned from Paris 2024.

Canoe Sprint.gamesContext: Canoe Sprint returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a longstanding discipline since 1936, featuring 12 medal events across men's kayak singles 1000m, doubles and fours 500m, canoe singles 1000m and doubles 500m, plus women's kayak singles 500m, doubles and fours 500m, canoe singles 200m and doubles 500m. The format follows a progression of heats, quarterfinals, semifinals, and finals over flatwater distances of 200m, 500m, and 1000m in kayak and canoe boats with one, two, or four paddlers. Compared to Paris 2024, the men's kayak singles shifts from 500m to 1000m while women's kayak singles moves from 200m to 500m, with other events maintaining their distances.

Cycling Road (Road Race).gamesContext: Road cycling road races return to the 2028 Summer Games as staple events alongside time trials, featuring two medal events: men's and women's road races. The format remains a single-day mass-start race over a challenging course for each gender. No major changes from Paris 2024 are announced.

Cycling Road (Time Trial).gamesContext: Cycling Road Time Trial returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a staple event since its debut in 1996 for men and 1992 for women. It features Men's and Women's Individual Time Trial events, each awarding one gold, one silver, and one bronze medal, for a total of two medal events. The format remains individual against-the-clock racing over a set distance, unchanged from Paris 2024. No major storylines or changes announced yet.
Cycling Road (Time Trial).parisRecap: Remco Evenepoel (BEL) dominated men's events at Paris 2024, winning gold in both the Individual Time Trial ahead of Filippo Ganna (ITA) and Wout van Aert (BEL), and the Road Race ahead of Valentin Madouas (FRA) and Christophe Laporte (FRA), achieving the rare road race and time trial double despite a late puncture. In the women's Individual Time Trial, Grace Brown (AUS) took gold over Anna Henderson (GBR) and Chloé Dygert (USA). Kristen Faulkner (USA) won the women's Road Race gold, with Marianne Vos (NED) silver and Lotte Kopecky (BEL) bronze.

Cycling Track.gamesContext: Track cycling returns to the 2028 Games with 14 medal events across men's and women's sprint, keirin, team sprint, team pursuit, madison, and omnium, maintaining the same program and formats as Paris 2024. Events feature multi-round structures including qualifying, rounds with repechages, quarterfinals, semifinals, and finals for individual disciplines, plus all-round omnium and points-based madison. Notable storylines include defending Paris 2024 dominance by nations like Netherlands in men's sprints and New Zealand in women's sprints.

BMX Freestyle.gamesContext: BMX Freestyle returns to the 2028 Summer Games after debuting in Tokyo 2020, featuring men's and women's park events in a qualification-to-finals format. Two medal events are contested, one per gender, with no changes announced from Paris 2024. Sessions are scheduled for July 28 qualification and July 29 finals.

BMX Racing.gamesContext: BMX Racing returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a staple event since its debut in 2008, featuring separate men's and women's competitions each with a single medal event. The format follows the established progression of quarterfinals, last chance runs, semifinals, and finals. Two medal events total, with no announced changes from Paris 2024.
BMX Racing.parisRecap: In Paris 2024, France achieved a historic podium sweep in the men's BMX Racing with Joris Daudet winning gold in 31.422 seconds, Sylvain André taking silver, and Romain Mahieu bronze, marking a turnaround from their Tokyo 2020 disappointments. Saya Sakakibara of Australia claimed gold in the women's event, followed by silver medalist Manon Veenstra of the Netherlands and bronze medalist Zoé Claessens of Switzerland. The competitions at Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines featured the standard format of heats advancing to quarterfinals, last chance races, semifinals, and finals.

Equestrian.gamesContext: Equestrian returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a longstanding discipline with all three disciplines: dressage, eventing, and jumping. The program features 6 medal events matching Paris 2024: individual and team competitions in each discipline. Events unfold over multiple sessions including qualifiers, team finals, and individual finals, hosted sequentially from July 14-29. No format changes from Paris 2024 are noted.

Rowing.gamesContext: Rowing returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with 14 medal events in traditional boat classes plus the debut of coastal rowing as a new discipline. All standard events shift to a condensed 1500m distance for a shorter, more intense format compared to the 2000m races at Paris 2024. Competition spans from July 15 with heats at Marine Stadium through finals by July 22, followed by coastal racing on July 24-25. Notable changes include the distance reduction and coastal addition to refresh the sport's appeal.

Rowing Coastal.gamesContext: Coastal Rowing debuts at the 2028 Summer Games as Beach Sprint Rowing, a new format alongside classic rowing, featuring short sprint races on coastal waters. The program includes multiple medal events across men's, women's, and mixed categories in solo and double sculls. This marks the first time rowing appears in two disciplines at the Games, with beach sprints emphasizing speed and agility in open water conditions.
Rowing Coastal.parisRecap: Coastal Rowing was not contested at Paris 2024.

Shooting (R/P).gamesContext: Shooting rifle and pistol events return to the 2028 Summer Games as a core sport with 15 medal events across individual and mixed team formats, including qualification stages followed by finals. The program maintains the structure from Paris 2024 with no major changes to events or formats announced. Shotgun events shift to a separate venue while rifle and pistol consolidate indoors.

Shooting (Shotgun).gamesContext: Shotgun shooting returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the same six medal events as Paris 2024: men's and women's trap and skeet, plus mixed team skeet. Events follow the standard format of multi-day qualification rounds leading to finals for top six competitors. No changes in events or formats are planned from Paris 2024. Medal events total six, with shotgun separated from rifle and pistol disciplines.

Sailing (D/S/M).gamesContext: Sailing returns to the 2028 Games with 10 medal events across windsurfing, kite, dinghy, skiff, and multihull classes, reducing from 10 events in Paris 2024 by dropping individual windsurfer and kiteboarding while retaining dinghy, skiff, and mixed multihull formats. Competition spans July 16-28 in a multi-day format of qualification series, followed by finals for select events. Dinghy, skiff, and multihull events feature tactical racing through Port of Los Angeles waters, with windsurfing and kite at a separate site.

Sailing (W/K).gamesContext: Sailing returns to the 2028 Summer Games with 10 medal events across windsurfing, kite, dinghy, skiff, and multihull classes, following the same format as Paris 2024. Windsurfing and kite events feature multi-day qualifiers leading to finals, while other classes use qualification series and medal races. Board events including men's and women's windsurfing and kiteboarding are scheduled first at Belmont Shore.

Trampoline Gymnastics.gamesContext: Trampoline Gymnastics returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the same format as prior editions, featuring men's and women's individual events each comprising a qualification round followed by a final. The program includes four medal events total, with no changes from Paris 2024. Qualification advances the top eight performers to finals based on execution and difficulty scores.

Rhythmic Gymnastics.gamesContext: Rhythmic Gymnastics returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the standard format of individual and group all-around competitions, featuring qualification and final sessions for each. The program includes two medal events: women's individual all-around and women's group all-around, unchanged from Paris 2024. Performances showcase routines with ribbon, hoop, ball, and clubs for individuals, and synchronized group exercises.
Rhythmic Gymnastics.parisRecap: In Paris 2024, Darja Varfolomeev of Germany won gold in women's individual all-around, with Boryana Kaleyn of Bulgaria taking silver and Sofia Raffaeli of Italy bronze. The women's group event saw People's Republic of China claim gold, followed by Israel in silver and Italy in bronze.

Artistic Swimming.gamesContext: Artistic Swimming returns to the 2028 Summer Games with five medal events: women's duet technical, women's duet free, open team technical, open team free, and open team acrobatic. The open team format continues from Paris 2024, featuring three routines per team: technical, free, and acrobatic. No major format changes are planned, maintaining the emphasis on synchronization, difficulty, and execution.

Triathlon.gamesContext: Triathlon returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the standard Olympic distance individual events for men and women, plus the mixed team relay. The program features three medal events, unchanged in format from Paris 2024. All events combine a 1.5 km swim in the ocean, 40 km bike on streets, and 10 km run on the beach.

Modern Pentathlon.gamesContext: Modern Pentathlon returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a continuous Olympic sport since 1912, featuring men's and women's individual events with semifinals and finals over four days from July 15-18. The format consists of a single-day competition per athlete encompassing fencing, swimming, riding, laser run, and a final shoot, structured as semifinals leading to finals. Two medal events are contested, with no changes from Paris 2024. It occupies the front of the schedule for maximum global viewership.

Mountain Bike.gamesContext: Mountain Bike returns to the 2028 Summer Games as a staple discipline with two medal events: Men's Cross-Country and Women's Cross-Country. Both feature individual cross-country races on a 4-6km twisting course with climbs, descents, drops, jumps, and varied terrain. The format remains unchanged from Paris 2024, maintaining the standard Olympic cross-country distance.

Open Water Swimming.gamesContext: Open Water Swimming returns to the 2028 Summer Games with the traditional men's and women's 10km individual races, offering two medal events. The format remains a single-loop coastal course similar to Paris 2024. No changes to events or structure are announced from the prior edition.
